debug_info.goal:
V : Type u_1
Ω : Type u_2
m : MeasurableSpace Ω
G : Ω → SimpleGraph V
p : ↑unitInterval
P : Measure Ω
hG : @SimpleGraph.IsBinomialRandom V Ω m G p P
⊢ AEMeasurable (fun ω => (G ω)ᶜ) P

failed_attempts:
by
    have h₁ : AEMeasurable (fun ω : Ω => (G ω)ᶜ) P := by
      have h₂ : Measurable (fun ω : Ω => (G ω)ᶜ) := by
        have h₃ : Measurable (fun ω : Ω => G ω) := hG.1.measurable
        have h₄ : Measurable fun ω : Ω => (G ω)ᶜ := by
          -- Prove that the complement is measurable
          have h₅ : Measurable fun (ω : Ω) => G ω := hG.1.measurable
          have h₆ : Measurable fun (ω : Ω) => (G ω)ᶜ := by
            -- Use the fact that the complement of a measurable graph is measurable
            refine' (measurable_comap_iff (by infer_instance)).mpr fun u v => _
            -- For each pair u, v, the adjacency function is measurable
            simp only [SimpleGraph.compl_def, SimpleGraph.adj_compl]
            have h₇ : Measurable fun (ω : Ω) => (G ω).adj u v := (h₅.measurable_iff_adj).2 u v
            have h₈ : Measurable fun (ω : Ω) => ¬(G ω).adj u v := by
              apply Measurable.not
              exact h₇
            exact h₈
          exact h₆
        exact h₄
      -- Convert the measurable function into a measurable function with respect to the measure P
      have h₃ : AEMeasurable (fun ω : Ω => (G ω)ᶜ) P := by
        exact h₂.aemeasurable
      exact h₃
    exact h₁
  sorry
